def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    std_mult = params['std_mult']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']
    
    # Calculate Bollinger Bands
    rolling_mean = df['close'].rolling(window=window).mean()
    rolling_std = df['close'].rolling(window=window).std()
    upper_band = rolling_mean + (rolling_std * std_mult)
    lower_band = rolling_mean - (rolling_std * std_mult)
    
    # Calculate RSI
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=rsi_window).rsi()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[(df['close'] > upper_band) & (rsi > rsi_threshold)] = -1
    signals[(df['close'] < lower_band) & (rsi < (100 - rsi_threshold))] = 1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: USD/CHF exhibits mean-reverting behavior when price deviates significantly from its moving average, with RSI confirming overbought/oversold conditions.
